scope_note: QA Rhind 2/n Unit Fraction cert [315]. Primary sources: Chace, A.B. (1927) The Rhind Mathematical Papyrus, Mathematical Association of America, Vol. 1-2, pp.7-13; Gillings, R.J. (1972) Mathematics in the Time of the Pharaohs, MIT Press, ISBN 978-0-486-24315-3, Ch.6 pp.45-80; Iverson, B. (1975-1996) Quantum Arithmetic vols 1-2 (QA-1, QA-2). QA mapping: the Rhind Papyrus (c.1550 BCE) 2/n table for odd n=3..101 (50 entries) is classified by orbit_family on (dr(n), dr(n), 9) where dr(n)=digital root=((n-1)%9)+1. Satellite/Singularity (3|n, 17 entries): ALL use 2/(3k)=1/(2k)+1/(6k); 2-term always. Cosmos (3 does not divide n, 33 entries): 2-term (12), 3-term (13), 4-term (8). CLAIM: (C1) all 50 Rhind sums equal 2/n as exact Python Fraction; no float. (C2) all 17 Satellite/Singularity n have exactly 2 unit fractions in the Rhind entry. (C3) for all 17 n=3k, Rhind denominators are exactly (2k, 6k); the 3-divisor formula 2/(3k)=1/(2k)+1/(6k) generates all Satellite entries identically. (C4) 4-term decompositions occur for exactly 8 Cosmos n {29,43,61,73,79,83,89,101}; zero Satellite or Singularity entry has 4 terms. (C5) Theorem NT: unit fraction notation, hieroglyphic form, and scribal layout are observer projections; orbit_family, Fraction sums, term count, and 3-divisor formula are the QA discrete claims. Builds on cert [314] (Egyptian Ennead Orbit Partition, mod-9 3-divisibility).
